7-maths-ch1.label: Ch 1: Large Number Around Us
7-maths-ch1.title: Class 7 Maths: Ch 1 - Large Numbers Around Us

questions.q: Find the value of the expression: 30 + 5 × 4
questions.explain: Multiplication is done first: 5 × 4 = 20, then 30 + 20 = 50. Without brackets, we evaluate the term 5 × 4 first before adding.

questions.q: What are the terms of the expression 83 – 14?
questions.explain: Subtracting a number is the same as adding its inverse: 83 – 14 = 83 + (−14). So the terms are 83 and −14.

questions.q: "Swapping the terms of an expression does not change its value" is called the:
questions.explain: This is the commutative property of addition, e.g., 6 + (−4) = (−4) + 6.

questions.q:
Irfan paid ₹100 for a biscuit packet (₹15) and toor dal (₹56). What is the change he gets?
Expression: 100 – (15 + 56)
questions.explain: First evaluate the bracket: 15 + 56 = 71. Then 100 – 71 = 29. Irfan gets ₹29 as change.

questions.q: Find the value of: 5 × (3 + 2) + 7 × 8 + 3
When brackets preceded by a negative sign are removed, the signs of the terms inside the brackets:
questions.explain: For example, 100 – (15 + 56) = 100 – 15 – 56. Both +15 and +56 change sign to −15 and −56 once the bracket is removed.

questions.q: Find the value of: 500 – (250 – 100)
questions.explain: First subtract inside the bracket: 250 – 100 = 150. Then 500 – 150 = 350. Note: this is NOT the same as 500 – 250 – 100.

questions.q: Using the distributive property, 14 × 10 – 6 × 10 can be written as:
questions.explain: The multiple of a difference is the same as the difference of the multiples: 14 × 10 – 6 × 10 = (14 – 6) × 10 = 8 × 10 = 80.

questions.q:
Hira has 28 coins in one bag and 35 in another. She gifts 10 coins from the second bag. Find the coins left:
28 + (35 – 10)
questions.explain: Since the bracket is not preceded by a negative sign, signs don't change: 28 + 35 – 10 = 63 – 10 = 53.

questions.q: Using 97 × 25 = (100 – 3) × 25, find the value of 97 × 25.
questions.explain: 97 × 25 = 100 × 25 – 3 × 25 = 2500 – 75 = 2425. This distributive trick makes mental multiplication faster.

7-maths-ch3.title: Class 7 Maths - Ch 3: A Peek Beyond the Point

questions.q: A screw's length is measured as 2 and 7 one-tenths cm. How is this written and read?
options: 2.7 cm, read as 'two and seven-tenth centimeters'
0.27 cm, read as 'zero point two seven'
questions.explain: The unit is divided into 10 equal parts, and 7 such parts are taken beyond 2 whole units, giving 2 and 7 one-tenths cm, written as 2.7 cm.

questions.q: Sonu's lower arm is 2 7/10 units and upper arm is 3 6/10 units. What is the total length of his arm?
questions.explain: Adding units and tenths separately: (2+3) + (7/10+6/10) = 5 + 13/10 = 5 + 1 + 3/10 = 6 3/10 units, since 13 one-tenths = 1 unit + 3 one-tenths.

questions.q: A sheet of paper is 8 9/10 units long and folded in half. Its length ends between 4 4/10 and 4 5/10. To measure it exactly, each one-tenth is split into 10 parts, giving units of:
questions.explain: Splitting each one-tenth into 10 equal parts creates 100 smaller parts in a unit, so each part measures 1/100 of a unit — a hundredth.

questions.q: In the decimal number 70.5, what does the digit 5 represent (place value)?
questions.explain: In 70.5, the digit 5 is in the tenths place, so it represents 5 × 1/10, since decimal notation extends the place value system to the right of the decimal point with each place 10 times smaller than the one to its left.

questions.q: How is 234 tenths written in decimal form?
254 g is written in kg as:
questions.explain: Since 1 g = 1/1000 kg, 254 g = 254/1000 kg = 0.254 kg (each gram is one-thousandth of a kilogram).

questions.q: Which decimal number is closest to 1: 0.9, 1.1, 1.01, 1.11?
questions.explain: Comparing distances from 1: 1.01 is 1/100 away from 1, while 0.9 is 10/100 away, 1.1 is 10/100 away, and 1.11 is 11/100 away. So 1.01 is closest to 1.

questions.q: Which decimal number is greater: 3.81 or 13.800?
questions.explain: We compare the most significant digits first. 13.800 has a '1' in the tens place while 3.81 has none (0 tens), so 13.800 is greater, regardless of the extra zeros in its decimal part.

questions.q: Priya needs 2.7 m of cloth and Shylaja needs 3.5 m. What is the total quantity of cloth needed, and how much more does Shylaja need compared to Priya?
questions.explain: Sum: 2.7 + 3.5 = 6.2 m. Difference: 3.5 – 2.7 = 0.8 m, found using the standard addition/subtraction procedure applied to decimals just like whole numbers.

questions.q: A message says the bus will arrive in 4.5 hours. A carpenter also mistakes 2.5 ft as 2 ft 5 inches instead of 2 ft 6 inches. What do these situations illustrate about decimals?
options: Decimals should always be rounded to whole numbers to avoid confusion
Decimal notation can be misread when mixed with non-decimal units like hours or feet-inches, since 0.5 doesn't always mean '5' of a smaller sub-unit in the same way
0.5 always equals half of any unit regardless of context
Decimal points should never be used for time or length measurements
questions.explain: 0.5 hours means 5 tenths of 60 minutes = 30 minutes, not '5' minutes or seconds; similarly 0.5 ft = 6 inches (since 1 ft=12 inches), not 5 inches. Confusing decimal fractions with non-decimal unit conversions (like feet-inches or hours-minutes) causes real errors, as shown in the door and bus examples.
